blocks.content: His mom opened the door and went completely still.
She grabbed him. Camera and all. He laughed and held her back and let her have the moment before he stepped back and lifted the camera.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I need you to say something. For Daisy.
His mom looked at the camera. Then at her son. The same look she'd been giving him his whole life — the one that said she saw more than he thought she did.
She was quiet for a moment. When she spoke it was carefully — the way she chose words when they mattered.

blocks.speaker: Kit's Mom
blocks.content: Daisy. You have been a part of this family since before either of you understood what that meant. My son has loved you his whole life. I have watched him try to be worthy of that — try to be the kind of man who earns what he's asking for.
She paused. Something moved across her face — something old and private.

blocks.speaker: Kit's Mom
blocks.content: He is not his father. I want you to know that. He chose differently. He has always chosen differently. And I am proud of him for it every single day.

blocks.speaker: Kit's Mom
blocks.content: She needs to know. You need to know I see it.
He brought the camera back up. Kept it steady.
He didn't trust himself to speak.
